Neisseria gonorrhoeae is a highly adaptable bacterium that poses significant challenges in treatment and prevention. One of the key reasons Neisseria gonorrhoeae can re-infect individuals after treatment is its ability to evade the immune response. This evasion allows the bacterium to persist in the host despite the presence of antibodies. Additionally, Neisseria gonorrhoeae can undergo genetic variation, which enables it to alter its surface proteins and resist the effects of antibiotics. These two factors—immune evasion and genetic variation—are critical in understanding why Neisseria gonorrhoeae continues to be a public health concern.
